title: Issue Record
description: One file per issue at .excn/issues/<uuid>-<slug>.json — the per-record form that supersedes the backlog.json collection for NEW issues (ADR-0011: the directory is the tracker, no manifest). This schema validates a SINGLE issue object, not the {schema_version, issues:[]} wrapper. The collection schema issue.schema.json is retained for legacy/archive validation only: backlog.json before migration and the closed sprint-<N>/sprint-<N>-issues.json partitions. New writes go through the writeRecord helper (src/bin/write-record.js), which mints the id and validates against THIS schema. id is a UUIDv7 for new records or a grandfathered legacy id (EXEC-NNN) on migrated ones; prd likewise accepts a legacy PRD-NNN or a UUIDv7. The widened id/prd patterns mirror UUIDV7_PATTERN in src/bin/write-policy.js — keep the UUIDv7 branch in sync.

id.description: Stable identifier, never reused: a UUIDv7 for new records or a grandfathered legacy id (e.g. EXEC-097) on migrated ones.

severity.description: P1 blocking, P2 significant, P3 low-priority.

scope.description: Which area(s) the fix lives in — project-defined.

classification.description: macro = architecture-level; local = tactical; pre-existing = predates current work.

actionable_now.description: false = tracking only, no sprint work planned.

prd.description: The PRD this issue traces to, null if filed directly: a legacy PRD-NNN or a UUIDv7.

acceptance_criteria.description: Verifiable criteria; the issue closes only when all hold.

slice_type.description: HITL = needs human interaction; AFK = completable without it.

depends_on.description: Issue IDs that must close first.
